problem text: A basket contains $4$ green apples and $50$ red apples. They are taken out one at a time, without replacement, until all $4$ green apples have been picked. What is the expected number of apples remaining in the basket?
problem solution: Observe that the $4$ green apples segment the $50$ red apples into $5$ sections. Without additional details about these sections, they are assumed to be of equal size on average. Thus, each section should contain about $10$ apples. Specifically, one section will follow the last green apple, so we anticipate $10$ red apples remaining after the final green apple is drawn.
problem hint: Apply a First Ace strategy where the aces represent Apples.
